svg.title: Aditya Dagar — Systems Engineer
svg.text: I work at the intersection of computation, intelligence, and design.
Building toward a deeper understanding of systems — whether they are made of
memory addresses, distributed machines, neural networks, or people. I care less
about frameworks and more about foundations.
A minimal HTTP/1.0 server built from POSIX sockets. An exercise in understanding
the protocol at the byte level — parsing raw requests, managing file descriptors,
and building reliable I/O without abstraction.
Extends the HTTP/1.0 work to concurrent connections using pthreads. Persistent
connections, thread pools, and careful synchronization. Where systems programming
A ground-up implementation of SHA-256 in C. The goal was not a working hash
function — it was understanding why the algorithm is constructed the way it is.
An AI-powered Life Operating System. An ongoing attempt to build software that
transforms intention into consistent action. Architecture-first, model-agnostic.
Simplicity is not the absence of complexity. It is complexity brought under control.
Every abstraction should justify its existence.
